properties.name: app.databricks.hikari.maximum-pool-size
properties.description: Max size of the Databricks Hikari pool. Databricks connections are expensive — keep small.

properties.name: app.databricks.hikari.minimum-idle
properties.description: Minimum number of idle connections Hikari maintains in the Databricks pool.

properties.name: app.databricks.hikari.connection-timeout
properties.description: Max wait (ms) for a connection from the Databricks pool. Accommodates SQL-warehouse cold-start (30-60s).

properties.name: app.databricks.hikari.idle-timeout
properties.description: Max time (ms) a connection may sit idle before eviction.

properties.name: app.databricks.hikari.max-lifetime
properties.description: Max lifetime (ms) of a connection. Kept just under the ~60-min OAuth M2M token TTL so connections are recycled before their bearer token expires. Do not raise above ~3500000.

properties.name: app.databricks.hikari.auto-commit
properties.description: Auto-commit state of pooled connections. Databricks has no real transactions; disabling avoids per-borrow toggle round trips.

properties.name: app.databricks.hikari.connection-test-query
properties.description: Query used to validate connections. Fallback for when the driver's Connection.isValid() is broken or slow.

properties.name: app.databricks.hikari.validation-timeout
properties.description: Max time (ms) the connection-validation query may run.

properties.name: app.databricks.hikari.keepalive-time
properties.description: Interval (ms) between keepalive pings on idle connections, preventing intermediate firewalls / load balancers from killing them.

properties.name: app.databricks.hikari.leak-detection-threshold
properties.description: If a borrowed connection is not returned within this window (ms), Hikari logs a leak warning. 0 disables.
